guardrails: do not modify Visual Signature semantics
do not implement platform in this step
do not invent missing generated_texts artifacts
mark report prose as render-time derived

architecture_summary.decision: Extend the existing FastAPI/Jinja local UI with a platform shell and read-only Visual Signature sections instead of replacing the scoring app with the static platform.
architecture_summary.reasoning: The existing UI already owns the New Brand Scan/input flow, queue, status page, scoring report route, reports list, brand history, SQLite access, and Brand3 visual style. Visual Signature should be added as a separate read-only navigation/review area.
architecture_summary.separation_principle: Initial Scoring remains the executable workflow. Visual Signature remains an evidence, review, calibration, and governance surface. The platform can present both, but must not pipe Visual Signature into scoring.

1_where_visual_signature_should_appear.recommendation: Add a dedicated Visual Signature area in the existing local UI navigation, plus per-run read-only links where a run has Visual Signature-related evidence.

placements.purpose: Visual Signature overview with evidence-only warning, readiness, governance status, captures, reviewer workflow, calibration, and corpus expansion cards.
Optional read-only badges linking to Visual Signature overview, not per-score integration.
Optional separate Visual Signature evidence links by brand/capture id when artifacts can be matched without altering scoring.
Optional separate section or link labeled Visual Signature Evidence, explicitly no scoring impact. Do not inject Visual Signature into score explanations.

1_where_visual_signature_should_appear.not_recommended: Do not put Visual Signature controls inside the scoring input form or scoring status pipeline.

2_new_brand_scan_flow_connection.recommendation: Keep the New Brand Scan/input flow exactly connected to existing scoring.
unchanged_execution: web/routes/analyze.py validates URL and writes web_requests
src.services.brand_service.run collects inputs, extracts features, scores, saves SQLite rows, writes output JSON
src.reports.renderer.ReportRenderer renders reports from SQLite snapshots
2_new_brand_scan_flow_connection.platform_constraint: The unified platform may link to the scan form and status/report pages, but must not call scoring directly from Visual Signature routes or platform data adapters.

dimension_scores.preferred_for_ui: SQLiteStore.get_run_snapshot(run_id), because it provides scores, features, evidence, run metadata, and audit context together.

rendered_dimension_prose.display_strategy: Surface the same derived report context used by the renderer. Label it as render-time derived and do not invent or persist generated_texts_per_dimension.
rendered_dimension_prose.safe_option: Use deterministic derivation from src.reports.derivation.build_report_base/build_report_context for platform summaries. Avoid provider-backed narrative generation in platform adapters.

separation_rules: Visual Signature routes read only examples/visual_signature artifacts and optional existing visual_signature raw inputs.
Visual Signature does not write to runs, scores, features, rubric files, report templates, or output scoring JSON.
Visual Signature does not alter composite_score, dimensions, confidence summaries, or report readiness.
Visual Signature evidence is shown as separate evidence/review status, not as score evidence.
Visual Signature review outcomes do not update scoring tables.
Visual Signature capture and reviewer tools remain governed by their existing scripts and generated artifacts.
ui_language: No provider execution from platform view
Read-only unless using explicitly existing scoring scan form

routes.purpose: Unified local dashboard landing page. Shows Initial Scoring and Visual Signature as separate top-level lanes.
Read-only scoring overview: latest runs, JSON outputs, report links, rubric dimensions, score summaries.
Read-only run detail using SQLite snapshot. Shows dimension_scores, features, evidence, rules/insights, and links to /r/{token} where available.
Visual Signature overview, readiness, governance, next steps, source artifact links.
Capture manifest, screenshots, raw vs clean/full-page previews, obstruction/dismissal status.
Review queue, pilot status, packet links, embedded or linked reviewer viewer.
Calibration manifest, summary, records, reliability, readiness.
Capability registry, runtime policy matrix, governance integrity, validation plan.
Corpus expansion manifest, pilot metrics, pending items.
Optional local-only artifact serving for images/JSON/HTML under allowlisted roots.

5_routes_templates_components.base_template_change: Add platform navigation links to web/templates/base.html.j2 footer/header, keeping existing scoring routes unchanged.

scoring_adapters.name: ScoringRunsAdapter
scoring_adapters.purpose: Latest runs, run detail snapshots, brand history, report token lookup.

scoring_adapters.name: ScoringOutputFilesAdapter
scoring_adapters.purpose: File-based exports, rendered report links, graceful fallback when SQLite rows are absent.

scoring_adapters.name: ReportContextAdapter
scoring_adapters.purpose: Build render-time derived score/prose context without provider-backed narrative calls.

visual_signature_adapters.name: VisualSignatureArtifactAdapter
visual_signature_adapters.purpose: Read manifests, summaries, readiness, governance, queue, and pilot metrics.

visual_signature_adapters.name: VisualSignatureMediaAdapter
visual_signature_adapters.purpose: Resolve screenshot previews, full-page/raw/clean links, reviewer packets, local viewer links.

visual_signature_adapters.name: ArtifactIntegrityAdapter
visual_signature_adapters.purpose: Gracefully report missing optional artifacts and block missing required artifacts without generating new data.

executable_existing.note: Existing scoring workflow remains executable because it is already the product's input flow.
Existing status page remains executable/read state from web_requests.

explicitly_not_executable_from_platform: Report rendering with provider-backed narrative generation

8_out_of_scope: Changing production/local report templates for scoring meaning
Using Visual Signature as a scoring input
Persisting Visual Signature reviewer results through the platform
Running capture/dismissal scripts from the platform
Adding a database for the platform
Changing capture behavior or runtime mutation policy

9_minimum_implementation_plan.title: Add read-only adapters
9_minimum_implementation_plan.details: Create platform/scoring and platform/visual_signature adapters that read SQLite, output files, report files, rubric source, and Visual Signature artifacts without executing pipelines.

9_minimum_implementation_plan.title: Add platform routes
9_minimum_implementation_plan.details: Add web/routes/platform.py and web/routes/visual_signature.py. Include routers in web/app.py. Existing scoring routes remain unchanged.

9_minimum_implementation_plan.title: Add Jinja templates
9_minimum_implementation_plan.details: Create templates for /platform, /platform/scoring, /platform/scoring/run/{run_id}, and Visual Signature subsections using existing Brand3 CSS and components.

9_minimum_implementation_plan.title: Surface scoring data
9_minimum_implementation_plan.details: Use SQLiteStore.get_run_snapshot for run detail. Show dimension_scores from scores and derived report context from src.reports.derivation, clearly labeled render-time derived.

9_minimum_implementation_plan.title: Surface Visual Signature data
9_minimum_implementation_plan.details: Read manifests and screenshots from examples/visual_signature. Show raw JSON in collapsed details. Link reviewer viewer/packets.

9_minimum_implementation_plan.title: Add tests
9_minimum_implementation_plan.details: Test routes render with scoring artifacts, with Visual Signature artifacts, and with optional missing artifacts. Test no provider calls and no scoring recomputation.

9_minimum_implementation_plan.title: Keep static platform optional
9_minimum_implementation_plan.details: Use the existing static platform as a local export/reference if useful, but do not make it the primary architecture over the FastAPI/Jinja app.
